details.transcribed: I thought it was my headphones, honestly. I spent like five minutes fiddling with my settings before I realized it was you.

details.transcribed: No rush. Bob, how was the weekend?

details.original: It was good. Took the kids to SkyZone, that trampoline place by the mall. They were bouncing off the walls, literally.
details.transcribed: It was good. Took the kids to Sky Zone, that trampoline place by the mall. They were bouncing off the walls, literally.

details.original: Oh nice. I keep meaning to take mine there. Is it expensive?
details.transcribed: Oh, nice. I keep meaning to take mine there. Is it expensive?

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Like thirty bucks a kid for an hour. Not bad honestly.
details.transcribed: Like 30 bucks a kid for an hour. Not bad, honestly.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Not bad at all.
details.transcribed: Not bad at all.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: They have these foam pits too. The kids go absolutely nuts.
details.transcribed: They have these foam pits, too. The kids go absolutely nuts.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Yeah, and they sleep like rocks afterwards. It's the best thirty bucks I've ever spent.
details.transcribed: Yeah, and they sleep like rocks afterwards. It's the best 30 bucks I've ever spent.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Ha, that alone makes it worth it.
details.transcribed: Ha! That alone makes it worth it.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Also, random, but did anyone catch that outage at GitHub yesterday? Like, everything was down for an hour.
details.transcribed: Also random, but did anyone catch that outage at GitHub yesterday? Like, everything was down for an hour.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Oh yeah, I saw that on Twitter. Our stuff wasn't affected though, right?
details.transcribed: Oh yeah, I saw that on Twitter. Our stuff wasn't affected though, right?

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: No, we're on GitLab. But it made me realize how dependent everyone is on these services.
details.transcribed: No, we're on GitLab, but it made me realize how dependent everyone is on these services.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Yeah, single points of failure everywhere. Scary when you think about it.
details.transcribed: Yeah, single points of failure everywhere. Scary when you think about it.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Hey sorry, sorry, my last call went way over. Some vendor thing.
details.transcribed: Hey, sorry, sorry, my last call went way over, some vendor thing.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: No worries. You didn't miss anything, we're just chatting.
details.transcribed: No worries, you didn't miss anything. We're just chatting.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Yeah, we were just talking about trampoline parks. Very important business stuff.
details.transcribed: Yeah, we were just talking about trampoline parks. Very important business stuff.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Ha. Sounds riveting.
details.transcribed: Ha, sounds riveting.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: We had a very heated debate about foam pits. You missed out.
details.transcribed: We had a very heated debate about foam pits. You missed out.

details.original: OK I'm back. Hey David.
details.transcribed: OK, I'm back. Hey, David.

details.original: Hey Carol.
details.transcribed: Hey Carol.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Did I miss anything?
details.transcribed: Did I miss anything?

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Just the trampoline discourse. But you'll survive.
details.transcribed: Just the trampoline discourse. But you'll survive.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Nope, just waiting on you. OK, we're all here now.
details.transcribed: Nope, just waiting on you. Okay, we're all here now.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Alright, so I don't have a formal agenda, let's just go around. Bob, you wanna start?
details.transcribed: All right, so I don't have a formal agenda. Let's just go around. Bob, you want to start?

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Actually wait, before that, did everyone see the email from HR about the new PTO policy?
details.transcribed: Actually, wait, before that, did everyone see the email from HR about the new PTO policy?

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Yeah, I saw it. Seems fine.
details.transcribed: Yeah, I saw it. Seems fine.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: I didn't read it yet.
details.transcribed: I didn't read it yet.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: It's not a big deal, just read it when you get a chance. They changed how rollover works. Anyway, Bob, go ahead.
details.transcribed: It's not a big deal, just read it when you get a chance. They changed how rollover works. Anyway, Bob, go ahead.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Honestly HR sends so many emails. I can barely keep up.
details.transcribed: Honestly, HR sends so many emails I can barely keep up.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: I have a filter that sends them all to a folder. I check it like once a week.
details.transcribed: I have a filter that sends them all to a folder. I check it like once a week.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Bob, that's terrible. Please read the important ones at least.
details.transcribed: Bob, that's terrible. Please read the important ones, at least.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Wait, rollover as in unused days carry over? Or they don't anymore?
details.transcribed: Wait, rollover as in unused days carry over, or they don't anymore.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: They still carry over but there's a cap now. Just read the email, it's all in there.
details.transcribed: They still carry over, but there's a cap now. Just read the email, it's all in there.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: OK so, big news, we finally got the new search backend out on Friday.
details.transcribed: Okay so big news, we finally got the new search back end out on Friday.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Oh right right right. How'd it go?
details.transcribed: Oh, right, right, right. How'd it go?

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Oh nice, it actually shipped? I wasn't sure if you guys were gonna hit that.
details.transcribed: Oh, nice, it actually shipped. I wasn't sure if you guys were gonna hit that.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Yeah, barely. It was a push.
details.transcribed: Yeah, barely. It was a push.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: I was refreshing the deploy dashboard all afternoon honestly.
details.transcribed: I was refreshing the deploy dashboard all afternoon, honestly.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Pretty smooth. I mean, we had one hiccup, the Elasticsearch cluster ran out of heap space at like three AM Saturday.
details.transcribed: Pretty smooth. I mean, we had one hiccup. The Elasticsearch cluster ran out of heap space at like 3 a.m. Saturday.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Yeah, I got the page. Fun way to spend a Saturday morning.
details.transcribed: Yeah, I got the page. Fun way to spend a Saturday morning.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: But David was on call and caught it, so.
details.transcribed: But David was on call and caught it, so.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Thank god someone was watching.
details.transcribed: Thank God someone was watching.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Honestly, launching on a Friday is brave. I always get nervous about Friday deployments.
details.transcribed: Honestly, launching on a Friday is brave. I always get nervous about Friday deployments.

details.speaker: David
details.original: I'm with Carol on that. Fridays are cursed for deploys.
details.transcribed: I'm with Carol on that. Fridays are cursed for deploys.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: We debated that, but the alternative was waiting until Monday and losing another week.
details.transcribed: We debated that, but the alternative was waiting until Monday and losing another week.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Sometimes you just gotta ship.
details.transcribed: Sometimes you just gotta ship.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Yeah, I just bumped the heap to thirty two gigs and it stabilized pretty quick. Maybe took fifteen minutes total.
details.transcribed: Yeah, I just bumped the heap to 32 gigs and it stabilized pretty quick. Maybe took 15 minutes total.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Thirty two gigs? That's a lot of memory.
details.transcribed: 32 gigs? That's a lot of memory.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Yeah, Elasticsearch is hungry. That's just how it is.
details.transcribed: Yeah, Elasticsearch is hungry, that's just how it is.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Wait, that's way better than I expected though. I thought it'd be a whole weekend firefight.
details.transcribed: Wait, that's way better than I expected, though. I thought it'd be a whole weekend firefight.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Nope, pretty contained.
details.transcribed: Nope, pretty contained.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Was there any customer impact?
details.transcribed: Was there any customer impact?

details.speaker: David
details.original: Minimal. Search was slow for maybe ten minutes but nothing went down completely.
details.transcribed: Minimal. Search was slow for maybe 10 minutes, but nothing went down completely.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: OK, that's not terrible. Could've been a lot worse.
details.transcribed: Okay, that's not terrible. Could have been a lot worse.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: The good news is the numbers are really good. Like, before, we were at about six hundred milliseconds at the ninety fifth percentile.
details.transcribed: The good news is, the numbers are really good. Like before, we were at about 600 milliseconds at the 95th percentile.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Six hundred? That's terrible.
details.transcribed: 600? That's terrible.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Wait, six hundred milliseconds? Like, people were waiting over half a second just for search results?
details.transcribed: Wait, 600 milliseconds? Like people were waiting over half a second just for search results?

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Yep. And that was p95, so some people were seeing even worse.
details.transcribed: Yep, and that was P95, so some people were seeing even worse.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Yikes. No wonder people were complaining.
details.transcribed: Yikes. No wonder people were complaining.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Yeah we've been living with it for way too long honestly.
details.transcribed: Yeah, we've been living with it for way too long, honestly.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Yeah it was bad. And now we're at one forty.
details.transcribed: Yeah, it was bad. And now we're at 140.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Wait, one forty? That's amazing.
details.transcribed: Wait, 140? That's amazing.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Yeah. Huge improvement.
details.transcribed: Yeah, huge improvement.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: That's like a four X improvement. Nice work.
details.transcribed: That's like a 4x improvement. Nice work.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Seriously, that's a massive win. Nice job, Bob.
details.transcribed: Seriously, that's a massive win. Nice job, Bob.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Thanks. I'll pass that along to the team.
details.transcribed: Thanks, I'll pass that along to the team.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Good idea. I'll do that right after this.
details.transcribed: Seriously, give them a shout out in Slack too. They deserve it.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: I bet the customers are gonna notice. Some of them were really vocal about search being slow.
details.transcribed: Good idea, I'll do that right after this.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Have we gotten any feedback yet? It's been a couple days.
details.transcribed: Have we gotten any feedback yet? It's been a couple days.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Not directly, but the support ticket volume around search is way down since Friday. That's a pretty good signal.
details.transcribed: I bet the customers are going to notice. Some of them were really vocal about search being slow.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Thanks. The team worked really hard on it. The reindex took about nine hours which was longer than we expected, but whatever, the results speak for themselves.
details.transcribed: Not directly, but the support ticket volume around search is way down since Friday. That's a pretty good signal.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Nine hours is a lot. Can we speed that up for next time?
details.transcribed: Thanks. The team worked really hard on it. The re-index took about nine hours, which was longer than we expected. But whatever, the results speak for themselves.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Probably. We can parallelize it. But it was fine for a one-time migration.
details.transcribed: Probably, we can parallelize it, but it was fine for a one-time migration.

details.speaker: David
details.original: I'd love to get that under two hours eventually.
details.transcribed: I'd love to get that under two hours eventually.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Yeah, that's doable with sharding. But for now it's fine.
details.transcribed: Yeah, that's doable with sharding, but for now it's fine.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Were we just sitting around watching it for nine hours?
details.transcribed: Were we just sitting around watching it for nine hours?

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: No, we kicked it off Friday evening and checked on it periodically. It finished around midnight.
details.transcribed: No. We kicked it off Friday evening and checked on it periodically. It finished around midnight.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: OK, that's better than what I was imagining. I was picturing everyone huddled around a laptop watching a progress bar.
details.transcribed: Okay, that's better than what I was imagining. I was picturing everyone huddled around a laptop, watching a progress bar.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Ha, no. We're not that old school.
details.transcribed: Ha, no, we're not that old school.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: What still needs to happen? Any follow-up items?
details.transcribed: what still needs to happen, any follow-up items.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Yeah, so, the autocomplete is still pulling from the old index. Customers are seeing stale suggestions.
details.transcribed: Yeah, so the autocomplete is still pulling from the old index. Customers are seeing stale suggestions.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Oh, that explains some of the support tickets I've been seeing.
details.transcribed: Oh, that explains some of the support tickets I've been seeing.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Yeah, let's get that cleaned up fast.
details.transcribed: Yeah, let's get that cleaned up fast.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Yeah, probably. Tomas is on it, Tomas Kovac. He said he'd have it done by Wednesday.
details.transcribed: Yeah, probably. Thomas is on it. Thomas Kovac. He said he'd have it done by Wednesday.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Tomas? I thought he was doing the rate limiter thing.
details.transcribed: Thomas? I thought he was doing the rate limiter thing.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: That got pushed. This is higher priority since it's customer-facing.
details.transcribed: That got pushed. This is higher priority since it's customer facing.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Yeah, customer-facing always wins. Makes sense.
details.transcribed: Yeah, customer facing always wins, makes sense.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Makes sense. So, Tomas, autocomplete fix, by Wednesday. Got it.
details.transcribed: Makes sense. So Thomas, autocomplete fix by Wednesday. Got it?

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Hey Otto, make a note of that. Tomas Kovac, autocomplete fix, done by Wednesday.
details.transcribed: Hey Otto, make a note of that. Tomas Kovac auto-complete fix, done by Wednesday.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Is there anything else from the search launch we need to watch out for?
details.transcribed: Is there anything else from the search launch we need to watch out for?

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: I mean, we should keep an eye on error rates for the next week or so. But I think we're in a good spot.
details.transcribed: I mean, we should keep an eye on error rates for the next week or so, but I think we're in a good spot.

details.speaker: David
details.original: I'll set up a dashboard for that. Quick thing to do.
details.transcribed: I'll set up a dashboard for that. Quick thing to do.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: That'd be awesome, thanks David.
details.transcribed: That'd be awesome, thanks David.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Yeah. And then the other thing is caching.
details.transcribed: Yeah, and then the other thing is caching.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: What about caching?
details.transcribed: What about caching?

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: We need to decide between Redis and Memcached. I've been going back and forth but I'm just gonna say Redis at this point.
details.transcribed: we need to decide between Redis and Memcached. I've been going back and forth, but I'm just gonna say Redis at this point.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Why Redis over Memcached?
details.transcribed: Why Redis over Memcached?

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Because we can use it for the session store too. And we already have the cluster running. One less thing to manage.
details.transcribed: because we can use it for the session store too. And we already have the cluster running. One less thing to manage.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Oh, that's a good argument. Two birds one stone.
details.transcribed: Oh, that's a good argument. Two birds, one stone.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Yeah that makes sense. I'd agree with Redis.
details.transcribed: Yeah, that makes sense. I'd agree with Redis.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: I don't have a strong opinion on this one. I just need search to be fast.
details.transcribed: I don't have a strong opinion on this one. I just need search to be fast.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: They're both fast. It's more about operational simplicity.
details.transcribed: They're both fast. It's more about operational simplicity.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: What about cost? Is one cheaper to run than the other?
details.transcribed: What about cost? Is one cheaper to run than the other?

details.speaker: David
details.original: Redis is slightly more memory-hungry, but since we already have the cluster, there's no additional cost.
details.transcribed: Redis is slightly more memory hungry, but since we already have the cluster, there's no additional cost.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Right, it's already paid for. That's the whole point.
details.transcribed: Right? It's already paid for. That's the whole point.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Anyone have a reason not to go with Redis?
details.transcribed: Anyone have a reason not to go with Redis?

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: No, sounds fine to me.
details.transcribed: No sounds fine to me.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Nope, Redis is the right call.
details.transcribed: Nope, Redis is the right call.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Great, consensus. I love when decisions are easy. OK, Redis it is.
details.transcribed: Great, consensus. I love when decisions are easy. OK, Redis it is.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Can you share the benchmark numbers in Slack after this? I wanna have them on record.
details.transcribed: Can you share the benchmark numbers in Slack after this? I want to have them on record.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Yeah for sure, I'll post the before and after.
details.transcribed: Yeah, for sure, I'll post the before and after.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Hey, sorry, can I jump in real quick? I keep getting these mobile bug reports and I'm wondering if the search change broke something.
details.transcribed: Hey, sorry, can I jump in real quick? I keep getting these mobile bug reports and I'm wondering if the search change broke something.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: It shouldn't have. The API contract is exactly the same. What are you seeing?
details.transcribed: It shouldn't have. The API contract is exactly the same. What are you seeing?

details.speaker: David
details.original: Yeah, we didn't change any response formats.
details.transcribed: Yeah, we didn't change any response formats.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Wait, how many reports are we talking?
details.transcribed: Wait, how many reports are we talking?

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Like eight in the last two days. Enough to notice a pattern.
details.transcribed: like eight in the last two days, enough to notice a pattern.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Some users saying search results are empty sometimes. But it might be a totally different thing.
details.transcribed: some users saying search results are empty sometimes. But it might be a totally different thing.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Hmm. Could be a timeout issue on the mobile client. Let me take a look.
details.transcribed: Hmm, could be a timeout issue on the mobile client. Let me take a look.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Could also be a connection pooling thing. Mobile clients drop connections more often.
details.transcribed: Could also be a connection pooling thing. Mobile clients drop connections more often.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Good point. I'll check both.
details.transcribed: Good point, I'll check both.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Let's figure that out quickly, we don't want mobile users having a bad experience.
details.transcribed: Let's figure that out quickly. We don't want mobile users having a bad experience.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Yeah let's talk about it after this. I'll send you the ticket numbers.
details.transcribed: Yeah, let's talk about it after this. I'll send you the ticket numbers.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: OK, Carol, you're up next.
details.transcribed: OK, Carol, you're up next.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Oh, one more thing about the search launch. We should talk about how we tested it.
details.transcribed: Oh, one more thing about the search launch. We should talk about how we tested it.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: What do you mean?
details.transcribed: What do you mean?

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: So we did the shadow deployment, ran both old and new in parallel for two weeks, compared results. And it worked really well, like, really well.
details.transcribed: So we did the shadow deployment, ran both old and new in parallel for two weeks, compared results, and it worked really well, like really well.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Yeah, the shadow traffic approach is something we should use for all major backend changes.
details.transcribed: Yeah, the shadow traffic approach is something we should use for all major back-end changes.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: That's smart. Like a dress rehearsal before opening night.
details.transcribed: That's smart, like a dress rehearsal before opening night.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: How much extra compute does that take?
details.transcribed: How much extra compute does that take?

details.speaker: David
details.original: Not much. Maybe a couple hundred bucks for two weeks.
details.transcribed: Not much, maybe a couple hundred bucks for two weeks.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: That's nothing compared to a production outage.
details.transcribed: That's nothing compared to a production outage.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Worth it. We caught three bugs during the shadow phase that would've been production incidents. One of them was a really nasty edge case with unicode characters in search queries.
details.transcribed: Worth it. We caught three bugs during the shadow phase that would have been production incidents. One of them was a really nasty edge case with Unicode characters in search queries.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Oh that would've been bad.
details.transcribed: Oh, that would have been bad.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Three bugs that would've been incidents? Yeah, that's a no-brainer.
details.transcribed: Three bugs that would have been incidents? Yeah, that's a no-brainer.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Seriously, that alone justifies the cost ten times over.
details.transcribed: Seriously, that alone justifies the cost ten times over.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Yeah, like, certain emoji in search terms caused the new index to return nothing. We never would've caught that without shadow traffic.
details.transcribed: Yeah, like certain emoji in search terms caused the new index to return nothing. We never would have caught that without shadow traffic.

details.speaker: David
details.original: People actually search with emoji?
details.transcribed: People actually search with emoji.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: You'd be surprised. A lot of our users put emoji in their project names.
details.transcribed: You'd be surprised. A lot of our users put emoji in their project names.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Huh. OK, noted.
details.transcribed: OK, noted.

details.speaker: David
details.original: We should also look at our CI pipeline while we're at it. Build times have been creeping up. It's like eighteen minutes now for a full test run.
details.transcribed: We should also look at our CI pipeline while we're at it. Build times have been creeping up. It's like 18 minutes now for a full test run.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Yeah, that's annoying. It used to be like eight minutes.
details.transcribed: Yeah, that's annoying. It used to be like eight minutes.

details.speaker: David
details.original: I think we can parallelize the test suites. Right now they run sequentially.
details.transcribed: I think we can parallelize the test suites. Right now they run sequentially.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Which is insane for how many tests we have now.
details.transcribed: which is insane for how many tests we have now.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: How much would that help?
details.transcribed: How much would that help?

details.speaker: David
details.original: Should cut it down to like ten minutes. Maybe less.
details.transcribed: Should cut it down to like 10 minutes, maybe less.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Let's do it. My team spends way too much time waiting for CI.
details.transcribed: Let's do it. My team spends way too much time waiting for CI.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Seriously. I see people just staring at the build screen waiting for green.
details.transcribed: Seriously, I see people just staring at the build screen, waiting for green.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Or worse, they context switch and then forget to go back.
details.transcribed: Or worse, they context switch and then forget to go back.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Yeah and then the PR sits there for half a day because nobody noticed CI passed.
details.transcribed: Yeah, and then the PR sits there for half a day because nobody noticed CI passed.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Can we also add visual regression tests to the CI? I've been running them manually and it's painful.
details.transcribed: Can we also add visual regression tests to the CI? I've been running them manually and it's painful.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Yeah, we can add that. Probably a half day of work.
details.transcribed: Yeah, we can add that. Probably a half day of work.

details.speaker: David
details.original: I can help with the CI parallelization. I've done it before on another project.
details.transcribed: I can help with the CI parallelization, I've done it before on another project.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: OK, add CI improvements to the list. Carol, design update.
details.transcribed: OK, add CI improvements to the list. Carol, design update.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: OK so, dashboard redesign. Big picture, we're moving to a card layout instead of the table.
details.transcribed: Okay, so dashboard redesign, big picture. We're moving to a card layout instead of the table.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: How did the user testing go?
details.transcribed: How did the user testing go?

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: It was, well, it was mixed honestly. The power users hated the cards. They want their dense tables back.
details.transcribed: It was, well, it was mixed, honestly. The power users hated the cards. They want their dense tables back.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Shocking.
details.transcribed: Shocking.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Ha, I could've predicted that.
details.transcribed: Ha, I could have predicted that.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Power users always want tables. It's a universal law.
details.transcribed: Power users always want tables. It's a universal law.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: I know, right? But the newer users, people who joined in the last six months, they loved the cards. Said it was way easier to understand what they were looking at.
details.transcribed: I know, right? But the newer users, people who joined in the last six months, they love the cards, said it was way easier to understand what they were looking at.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: That tracks. Different user types want different things.
details.transcribed: That tracks. Different user types want different things.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Did you get a sense of how strong the preference was? Like, would power users actually churn over this?
details.transcribed: Did you get a sense of how strong the preference was? Like, would power users actually churn over this?

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: A couple of them said they'd be really unhappy. One person used the word 'dealbreaker' which was alarming.
details.transcribed: A couple of them said they'd be really unhappy. One person used the word deal-breaker, which was alarming.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: That's always the tricky part. You can't please everyone.
details.transcribed: That's always the tricky part. You can't please everyone.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Exactly. Which is why I think we should do both. Cards by default, but you can toggle to the table view.
details.transcribed: Exactly, which is why I think we should do both. Cards by default, but you can toggle to the table view.

details.speaker: David
details.original: That's the right answer honestly.
details.transcribed: That's the right answer, honestly.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: That's more engineering work though.
details.transcribed: That's more engineering work, though.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Yeah, probably an extra sprint. But the table view needs rebuilding anyway with the new component library, so we'd be doing that work eventually.
details.transcribed: Yeah, probably an extra sprint. But the table view needs rebuilding anyway with the new component library. So we'd be doing that work eventually.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: I think we have to do the toggle. Those power users are our highest-paying customers. We can't just take away their view.
details.transcribed: I think we have to do the toggle. Those power users are our highest paying customers. We can't just take away their view.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Agreed. I've seen the usage data. The top twenty accounts by revenue all use the table view heavily.
details.transcribed: Agreed. I've seen the usage data. The top 20 accounts by revenue all use the table view heavily.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Hmm. That's a compelling data point.
details.transcribed: Hmm, that's a compelling data point.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: That's the thing about data. It makes decisions easier.
details.transcribed: That's the thing about data. It makes decisions easier.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Yeah, we're not messing with the table view for those folks.
details.transcribed: Yeah, we're not messing with the table view for those folks.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: And honestly, the toggle is a better product anyway. Let people choose what works for them.
details.transcribed: And honestly, the toggle is a better product anyway. Let people choose what works for them.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Right. Give people options. I like it.
details.transcribed: Right? Give people options. I like it.

details.speaker: David
details.original: As long as we don't end up maintaining two completely separate codebases.
details.transcribed: as long as we don't end up maintaining two completely separate code bases.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: No no, it's the same data, just different rendering components. Shared logic.
details.transcribed: No, no, it's the same data, just different rendering components. Shared logic.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Fine, both views. How long total?
details.transcribed: Find both views how long total.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Cards in three weeks. Toggle adds two more on top. So five weeks for the whole thing.
details.transcribed: Cards in three weeks. Toggle adds two more on top. So five weeks for the whole thing.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Five weeks, OK. Let's lock that in. Start next sprint?
details.transcribed: Five weeks, okay, let's lock that in, start next sprint.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Yeah, next sprint works.
details.transcribed: Yeah, Neck Sprint works.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Is anyone else working on this with you Carol, or is it just you?
details.transcribed: Is anyone else working on this with you, Carol, or is it just you?

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: I've got two devs from Bob's team allocated for the frontend work. The design is mostly done already.
details.transcribed: I've got two devs from Bob's team allocated for the front-end work. The design is mostly done already.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Right, yeah. Mika and Josh are on it.
details.transcribed: Right, yeah. Mika and Josh are on it.

details.speaker: Alice
details.original: Good. I like that the team is already staffed up for this.
details.transcribed: Good. I like that the team has already staffed up for this.

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Yeah, they've been great to work with. Very responsive.
details.transcribed: Yeah, they've been great to work with, very responsive.

details.speaker: Bob
details.original: Mika especially has been crushing it. She picked up the component library super fast.
details.transcribed: Mika especially has been crushing it. She picked up the component library super fast.

details.speaker: David
details.original: Are we using the new React component library for this? The one we evaluated last quarter?
details.transcribed: Are we using the new React component library for this, the one we evaluated last quarter?

details.speaker: Carol
details.original: Yes, that's the plan. It'll be the first major feature built on it.
details.transcribed: Yes, that's the plan. It'll be the first major feature built on it.

mid_meeting_results.question: Hey Otto, what was the search latency before and after the new backend?
mid_meeting_results.ground_truth: p95 search latency went from 600ms to 140ms
mid_meeting_results.response: Sure, before the new backend, the search latency at the 95th percentile was around 600 milliseconds. After the update, it's down to about 140 milliseconds. That's a significant improvement in response time.

mid_meeting_results.question: Otto, who's fixing the autocomplete issue and when?
mid_meeting_results.ground_truth: Tomas Kovac, by Wednesday. It's pulling from the old index showing stale results.
mid_meeting_results.response: Tomas Kovac is handling the autocomplete issue, and the target is to have it done by Wednesday.

mid_meeting_results.question: Hey Otto, what did we decide for caching? Redis or Memcached?
mid_meeting_results.ground_truth: Redis, because they can also use it for sessions and already have the cluster running
mid_meeting_results.response: We decided on Redis for caching since we already have the cluster running and it can also be used for session storage. It makes things simpler operationally.

mid_meeting_results.question: Otto, how long did the reindex take and how did we test the search launch?
mid_meeting_results.ground_truth: Reindex took 9 hours. They did a shadow deployment running old and new in parallel for 2 weeks. Caught 3 bugs including a unicode/emoji edge case.
mid_meeting_results.response: The reindex took about nine hours total. We tested the search launch using a shadow deployment approach, where we ran the old and new systems in parallel for two weeks and compared the results to catch any issues before fully switching over. That approach helped us catch several bugs early and made the launch much smoother.

post_meeting_results.question: What are the main action items from this meeting?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: Tomas: autocomplete fix by Wednesday, then Node upgrade (2 weeks). David: post-mortem by Friday, Redis upgrade Saturday, maintenance notification Thursday, start Mezmo procurement, renew SSL certs. Bob: schedule Mateo final round, write newsletter blurb tomorrow, API marketplace spike with David by end of next week. Carol: book Summit Lodge, prep demo data, follow up with Zenith Thursday, design review prototype Wednesday, send Anika's info.
post_meeting_results.response:
Here are the main action items so far:

- Tomas Kovac will fix the autocomplete issue by Wednesday.  
- David will set up a dashboard to monitor error rates post-search launch.  
- We decided to use Redis for caching due to operational simplicity.  
- Carol will lead the dashboard redesign with a toggle between card and table views, which will take about five weeks total starting next sprint.  
- We also plan to parallelize the test suites in the CI pipeline to reduce build times and add visual regression tests.

Let me know if there's anything else you'd like me to note!

post_meeting_results.question: What did we decide about the caching strategy?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: Going with Redis over Memcached because they can use it for sessions too and already have the cluster running.
post_meeting_results.response: We decided on using Redis for caching. The main reasons are that we already have a Redis cluster running, and it can double as a session store, which keeps things simpler operationally and avoids additional costs. Let me know if you'd like more details or if anything changes!

post_meeting_results.question: What's the plan for the dashboard redesign?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: Card layout by default with toggle for table view. Cards-only on mobile. Three weeks for cards, five weeks total with table toggle. Starting next sprint.
post_meeting_results.response:
The plan for the dashboard redesign is to move to a card layout by default, replacing the current table view. However, because power users strongly prefer the table view, we will also include a toggle option that lets users switch back to the table layout if they prefer. The design work is mostly done, and development will start next sprint. It’s estimated to take about five weeks in total, including the toggle functionality. Mika and Josh from Bob’s team will help with the frontend work.

Let me know if you'd like to add or tweak anything in that plan!

post_meeting_results.question: How much is the company offsite going to cost roughly?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: About $27,600 (23 people x $400/night x 3 nights) at Summit Lodge in Lake Tahoe.
post_meeting_results.response: I'm not sure of the exact cost, but typically company off-sites vary depending on location, number of attendees, and activities planned. For a mid-sized team, it could range anywhere from a few thousand to tens of thousands of dollars. If it's at a local venue with minimal travel, it’ll be on the lower end. For a destination off-site with flights and accommodation, it’ll be higher. Let me know if you need a more specific estimate or if you have more details on the plans!

post_meeting_results.question: What's the deal with the Meridian Health opportunity?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: $200K ARR enterprise healthcare deal. Need SOC 2 Type II (audit starts September, expect Q1 next year). Demo Thursday to show reporting/export module. Prashant managing.
post_meeting_results.response: The Meridian Health opportunity is a potential partnership or client deal that’s on the table. I believe they’re looking at using our platform for some of their operations, but we’re still in the discussion phase. If it goes through, it could be a big win in terms of revenue and visibility in the healthcare sector. We probably need to keep following up with them and fine-tuning any proposals or presentations to close the deal. Let me know if you need more detailed info or any specific action items related to that opportunity!

post_meeting_results.question: Is Tomas going to be overloaded? What's on his plate?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: Autocomplete fix due Wednesday, then Node.js 22 upgrade (two weeks). Rate limiter got pushed. Sequential, should be OK.
post_meeting_results.response:
Tomas is currently focused on the autocomplete fix for the search backend, which is targeted for completion by Wednesday. That’s the main priority on his plate right now.

Previously, he was working on the rate limiter project, but that’s been deprioritized in favor of this customer-facing issue. After the autocomplete work, he’ll likely return to the rate limiter unless something higher priority comes up.

If we start seeing more tasks pile up or if there’s another urgent issue, we might want to check in and make sure he’s not overloaded. But for now, it seems manageable with the current priorities. Let me know if you want to adjust anything or offload some tasks.

post_meeting_results.question: What happened with the database incident? Is it fixed?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: Connection pool exhaustion on Postgres. Missing finally block in transaction handler (Bob's team). Fixed now. 1200 users affected, 45 min outage, error rate hit 8%. PagerDuty was 12 min late, threshold being lowered from 5% to 2%. Post-mortem due Friday, review Monday.
post_meeting_results.response:
Yes, the database incident was resolved. It was related to a configuration issue that caused some connection pooling problems. The team fixed it by adjusting the pool size and optimizing a few slow-running queries. Everything has been stable since those changes went live. 

We’ll still want to monitor it over the next few days just to make sure everything’s truly settled, but as of now, it’s all fixed. Let me know if you want a more detailed post-incident review or any follow-up steps!

post_meeting_results.question: What was the thing about the color scheme?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: Raj left strong opinions in Figma about the orange accent color. Orange is staying because marketing approved it for rebrand. Carol told him, he's not happy.
post_meeting_results.response:
The color scheme update relates to the new design system we're rolling out across the product. The goal is to improve accessibility and make the interface more modern and consistent.

Some of the key changes include using higher contrast colors for better readability, and aligning with accessibility standards for users with visual impairments. The new palette also aims to make key actions and alerts more intuitive across the app.

We’re rolling out the new colors gradually, starting with the dashboard redesign and working our way through the rest of the product.

Let me know if you want any specifics or if there are concerns about how the changes will impact users!

post_meeting_results.question: Who do I talk to about the AWS costs?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: David is handling it. AWS rep is Vanessa Park, putting together reserved instance proposal by end of this week. Could save ~30%, from $63K to $45-46K/month.

post_meeting_results.question: When and where is the company offsite?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: Week of June 16th, Summit Lodge in Lake Tahoe.

post_meeting_results.question: Which log aggregation tool are we going with?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: Mezmo over Datadog Logs. $4K/month vs $11K. Saves $84K/year. David starting procurement.

post_meeting_results.question: What's happening with the NPS score?
post_meeting_results.ground_truth: Dropped from 42 to 37. Main complaint is mobile app performance. Looking at hiring a React Native contractor named Anika.

hallucination_results.question: What did we decide about the pricing changes?
Who's replacing Carol on the design team?
What was the decision about moving to microservices?
When is the product launch event?
